name: Kylian Mbappé
description: Kylian Mbappé is widely regarded as the best player in the world. A lightning-quick forward with extraordinary finishing ability, he has been France's talisman since a breakout 2018 World Cup where he became the first teenager since Pelé to score in a World Cup final. Now at Real Madrid, Mbappé enters 2026 hungry for the one trophy that has so far eluded him.

name: Vinicius Júnior
description: Vinicius Júnior burst onto the world stage at Real Madrid and has become one of the most exciting wingers in football. His explosive pace, creativity, and flair have made him Brazil's number one attacking threat. The 2026 World Cup on North American soil represents his opportunity to lead Brazil to a record sixth title.

name: Hakim Ziyech
description: Hakim Ziyech is the creative heartbeat of Morocco's historic 2022 generation. A technically gifted left-footed playmaker with a wand of a left foot, he dictates the tempo for the Atlas Lions. Despite a complicated club career, Ziyech remains decisive at international level and will be crucial to Morocco's 2026 ambitions.

name: Lionel Messi
description: Lionel Messi is the greatest footballer of all time. The Argentine captain finally claimed his World Cup glory in 2022, cementing his legacy with a breathtaking final against France. Now in the twilight of his career at Inter Miami, a home-soil 2026 World Cup could be Messi's farewell on the grandest stage.

name: Christian Pulisic
description: Christian Pulisic is the face and captain of the United States national team. Known as 'Captain America', the AC Milan winger is the most talented American to play the game. The 2026 World Cup on home soil is the moment Pulisic was born for — a chance to inspire a nation and announce the USMNT as a genuine force.

name: Pedri
description: Pedri is the most gifted central midfielder of his generation. The Barcelona maestro plays with a maturity and intelligence that belies his age, controlling tempo and threading through balls with ease. A key cog in Spain's Euro 2024 triumph, Pedri is the heir to the great Spanish midfield tradition.
